subtitle: Multi-Tenant DBaaS for Red Hat OpenShift
intro: Enterprises running modern applications on Red Hat OpenShift struggle to deliver databases quickly. They must maintain security, governance, and operational control, but are often forced to choose between restrictive cloud-managed services or complex manual Kubernetes operations. KubeDB delivers a cloud-native Database-as-a-Service (DBaaS) platform directly on Red Hat OpenShift, enabling platform teams to standardize and govern production-ready databases across hybrid and air-gapped environments while giving developers self-service access through Kubernetes-native workflows.

items.name: Lower operational overhead
items.description: KubeDB standardizes database provisioning, upgrades, scaling, and recovery through declarative workflows, eliminating custom automation and reducing operational risk.

items.name: DBaaS without cloud lock-in
items.description: By running entirely inside Red Hat OpenShift, KubeDB delivers DBaaS consistently across on-premises, public cloud, sovereign, and air-gapped environments.

items.name: Predictable cost and storage flexibility
items.description: KubeDB works with existing enterprise storage platforms and Kubernetes storage classes, letting organizations optimize for performance, resilience, and cost while keeping full data ownership.

items.name: Built-in governance and multi-tenancy
items.description: Platform teams can enforce security, access controls, resource limits, and lifecycle policies, enabling secure multi-tenant DBaaS at scale.

items.name: Accelerated developer productivity
items.description: Developers gain self-service access to production-ready databases through Kubernetes-native interfaces, removing ticket-based provisioning.

items.name: Native Red Hat OpenShift integration
items.description: First-class OpenShift platform service using Kubernetes APIs and declarative configuration to manage databases as governed, reusable services.

items.name: Automated database lifecycle management
items.description: Provisioning, upgrades, scaling, and failover are driven by policy, ensuring consistency across clusters and environments.

items.name: Multi-database platform support
items.description: Supports SQL, NoSQL, caches, and message brokers from a single DBaaS control plane.

items.name: Integrated data protection and availability
items.description: Built-in backup, restore, and high-availability mechanisms reduce reliance on external, database-specific tooling.

items.name: Centralized governance
items.description: Operate distributed Red Hat OpenShift environments across clouds and data centers with centralized policy and local execution.
